mission.title: Install and Configure Litmus on Kubernetes
mission.description: Litmus is an open-source Chaos Engineering platform that allows SREs and developers to identify weaknesses and potential outages in their infrastructure by inducing chaos tests in a controlled manner. Installing Litmus enables teams to practice Chaos Engineering effectively within their Kubernetes environments.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Litmus Helm repository to your local Helm configuration using the following command:
```bash
helm repo add litmuschaos https://litmuschaos.github.io/litmus-helm/
```

steps.title: Update the Helm repository
steps.description:
Update your Helm repositories to ensure you have the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Litmus using Helm
steps.description:
Install the Litmus Helm chart in a new namespace called 'litmus':
```bash
helm install litmus litmuschaos/litmus --namespace litmus --create-namespace --version 3.26.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all Litmus pods are running correctly:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litmus
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Litmus release from your cluster:
```bash
helm uninstall litmus --namespace litmus
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with Litmus:
```bash
kubectl delete crd chaosengines.litmuschaos.io
kubectl delete crd chaosexperiments.litmuschaos.io
kubectl delete crd chaosresults.litmuschaos.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Litmus namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the Litmus namespace to clean up remaining resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace litmus
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your current Litmus state by exporting the existing CRDs:
```bash
kubectl get chaosengines -n litmus -o yaml > chaosengines-backup.yaml
kubectl get chaosexperiments -n litmus -o yaml > chaosexperiments-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Litmus
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Litmus Helm chart to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade litmus litmuschaos/litmus --namespace litmus --version 3.26.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that all Litmus pods are running correctly after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litmus
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
If you see pods in a Pending state, check for resource availability:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n litmus
``` 
Ensure your cluster has enough resources (CPU, memory) available.

troubleshooting.title: Litmus Operator not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Litmus Operator pod is not starting, check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs <litmus-operator-pod-name> -n litmus
``` 
Look for any error messages that may indicate configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Chaos experiments failing
troubleshooting.description:
If your chaos experiments are failing, check the status of the ChaosEngine:
```bash
kubectl get chaosengine <chaosengine-name> -n litmus -o yaml
``` 
Review the events section for any errors.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Litmus will have all pods running in the 'litmus' namespace, and you will be able to create and execute chaos experiments. You should also be able to verify the setup through the Litmus UI or CLI.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Kubernetes cluster access and the Helm CLI installed.
